Class Sim

java.lang.Object
sims.entity.Sim
public class Sim extends Object
Represents a Sim character in the Sims world. A Sim has attributes such as name, age, career, needs, relationships, financial status, and current location. Sims can perform activities, manage their needs, earn money, and interact with other Sims.

This class provides methods to update needs, manage relationships, handle career progression, and simulate autonomous behavior through activities and decay mechanics.

  • Constructor Details

    • Sim

      public Sim(String name, int UUID, int age)
      Constructs a new Sim with the specified name, UUID, and age.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the Sim
      UUID - the unique identifier of the Sim
      age - the age of the Sim

  • Method Details

    • setSkill

      public void setSkill(Map<String, SkillManager> skillMap)
      Assigns a skill map to the Sim.

      This method replaces the Sim’s current skillMap with the provided mapping of skill categories to their corresponding SkillManager. Each entry in the map represents a skill domain (e.g., Hunger, Hygiene, Energy, Bladder, Fun, Social) and is managed by its own SkillManager instance.

      Usage:

      Mapinvalid input: '<'String, SkillManager> skills = SimFactory.createSkills();
sim.setSkill(skills);

      This ensures that the Sim has a structured skill system in place, allowing activities, careers, and upgrades to interact with and improve the Sim’s abilities across different need categories.

      Parameters:
      skillMap - a mapping of skill categories to their SkillManager objects

    • getActivityEnd

      public int getActivityEnd()
      Returns:
      the time when the current activity ends

    • getAge

      public int getAge()
      Returns:
      the age of the Sim

    • getName

      the unique identifier of the Sim

    • getNeeds

      public Map<String,need> getNeeds()
      Returns:
      the dictionary of needs for this Sim

    • getBank

      public double getBank()
      Returns:
      the Sim's bank balance

    • updateBank

      public void updateBank(double cost)
      Updates the Sim's bank balance by subtracting a cost.
      Parameters:
      cost - the amount to subtract

    • addBank

      public void addBank(double value)
      Increases the Sim’s bank balance by the specified value.

      This method adds the given amount to the Sim’s current bank field, representing the Sim’s available funds. It is typically used when the Sim earns money through activities such as working, selling items, or receiving rewards.

      Usage Example:

      sim.addBank(500.0); // Adds 500 to the Sim’s bank balance

      Negative values may also be passed to represent expenses or deductions, though this should be handled carefully to avoid unintended results.

      Parameters:
      value - the amount to add to the Sim’s bank balance

    • addRelationship

      public void addRelationship(int uuid, Relationship relationship)
      Adds a relationship to this Sim.
      Parameters:
      uuid - the UUID of the other Sim
      relationship - the relationship object

    • getRelationshipMap

      public Map<Integer, Relationship> getRelationshipMap()
      Returns:
      the map of relationships

    • updateNeeds

      public void updateNeeds(String need, double value)
      Updates a need or salary for this Sim. If the need is not "Salary", adjusts the need value. If the need is "Salary", updates the bank balance and career progression.
      Parameters:
      need - the need to update
      value - the value to adjust

    • setNeeds

      public void setNeeds(Map<String,need> needDict)
      Sets the dictionary of needs for this Sim.
      Parameters:
      needDict - the map of needs

    • getLocation

      public Loc getLocation()
      Returns:
      the current location of the Sim

    • setCurrentLocation

      public void setCurrentLocation(Loc currentLocation)
      Sets the current location of the Sim.
      Parameters:
      currentLocation - the new location

    • getHome

      public Home getHome()
      Returns:
      the home associated with this Sim

    • setHome

      public void setHome(Home home)
      Sets the home associated with this Sim.
      Parameters:
      home - the new home

    • getCareer

      public Career getCareer()
      Returns:
      the career of the Sim

    • setCareer

      public void setCareer(Career career)
      Sets the career of the Sim.
      Parameters:
      career - the new career

    • setActivityEnd

      public void setActivityEnd(int duration, int time)
      Sets the end time of the current activity.
      Parameters:
      duration - the duration of the activity
      time - the current world time

    • getSkillMap

      public Map<String, SkillManager> getSkillMap()
      Retrieves the mapping of skill names to their corresponding SkillManager instances.

      This method provides access to the internal skill registry, where each skill is identified by a String key (e.g., "Cooking", "Fitness", "Logic") and managed by a SkillManager object that tracks its level and XP.

      Usage Example:

      Mapinvalid input: '<'String, SkillManager> skills = sim.getSkillMap();
SkillManager cookingSkill = skills.get("Cooking");
System.out.println("Cooking level: " + cookingSkill.getLevel());

      The returned map may be empty if no skills have been initialized yet. It can grow dynamically as new skills are added to the Sim.

      Returns:
      a Map where keys are skill names and values are SkillManager objects

    • autoPlay

      public void autoPlay(String need, int time)
      Automatically performs an activity that satisfies a given need. The Sim moves to the appropriate home location and executes the activity.
      Parameters:
      need - the need to satisfy
      time - the current world time

    • performDecay

      public void performDecay(String need, Sim currentSim, int time)
      Applies decay to the Sim's needs. If a need falls below its threshold and the Sim is not currently busy, the Sim will automatically perform an activity to satisfy that need.
      Parameters:
      need - the need to exclude from decay
      currentSim - the Sim currently being updated
      time - the current world time
